steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: P6 actually rated abstract and cubist equally. The question asks which graph data would illustrate a case where the model's predictions for P6 diverged from that reality.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — model predicted P6 would rate abstract higher than cubist: This picks a specific direction of divergence not established by the premise — we only know the actual ratings were equal, not which direction the model erred. ✗
  • Choice B — model predicted P6's ratings would be equal: This would mean the model matched P6's actual behavior, not diverged from it. ✗
  • Choice C — model predicted P6 would rate cubist higher than abstract: Like A, this asserts a specific direction not supported by the premise alone. ✗
  • Choice D — model predicted P6's ratings would differ: If the model predicted different ratings for abstract vs. cubist while P6 actually rated them equally, that demonstrates a clear mismatch between prediction and reality. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
